title: Bulbostylis afromicrocephala
taxonomy: Bulbostylis afromicrocephala is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Bulbostylis, which is a group of sedges that are native to tropical Africa. The family Cyperaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes many species of sedges, rushes, and spikes. Bulbostylis afromicrocephala can be further classified within the subfamily Cyperoideae, which is a subgroup of Cyperaceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogeny.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
